user_content: I'm trying to plan a trip to Kyoto or Osaka during the Golden Week holiday in May, but I'm not sure what to expect. Can you tell me a bit about what Golden Week is and what I should prepare for? By the way, I've been getting more comfortable with my daily commute to Roppongi for my English teaching job, which I started about 4 months ago.

I've been thinking about getting a new backpack for my upcoming trip to the Eastern Sierra, and I was wondering if you could recommend some good options for a lightweight backpack that's suitable for 3-4 day trips? By the way, I just got back from a day hike to Muir Woods National Monument with my family today, and it was amazing!

We saw some bear tracks near Point Lobos State Natural Reserve, and it was a bit surprising because we didn't expect to see them that close to the coast. We also saw some signs of bears digging near the trailheads, but we didn't actually see any bears. 

Our road trip was amazing, though! We loved the scenic drives along Highway 1, and the views of the coastline were just stunning. We also spent some time in Monterey, visiting Cannery Row and the Monterey Bay Aquarium, which were both really interesting. And our Airbnb in Pacific Grove was super cute and cozy.

I'm planning a trip to Sequoia National Park and was wondering if you could recommend some good hiking trails for a day hike. By the way, I've had some experience with long drives and hiking in national parks, like when I spent two nights at the Yosemite Valley Lodge during my road trip to Yosemite National Park.
I've been thinking about buying a new backpack for my upcoming trips, do you have any recommendations for a lightweight backpack that's suitable for 3-4 day trips?

Can I also get some information on the best time of day to visit the General Sherman Tree and the Congress Trail to avoid the crowds? I've experienced similar crowded situations during my road trip to Yosemite National Park, and I'd like to plan ahead.

Hi Jo, thanks for reaching out! Who would this meeting be with?

I've been collecting stamps for three months now, and I've already got over 200 unique stamps from different countries. Just last week, I received a package with 50 new stamps, including a rare 1962 Jamaican stamp that I've been searching for months. I've also been focusing on collecting stamps featuring animals, particularly birds and big cats.

That's really helpful, thanks for the info. I was thinking of staying in an Airbnb in Kyoto, but now I'm worried about the crowds and prices. Do you think it's worth considering a smaller town or city nearby, like Nara or Himeji, instead? I've been trying to improve my Japanese language skills for my students at the language school in Roppongi, but I'm not sure how much I'll be able to communicate with locals in these smaller towns.
I was just thinking about bear safety because we saw some signs of bears on our road trip to Big Sur and Monterey, and I'm always a bit more cautious after seeing that.
I'm also considering getting a new tent for my Eastern Sierra trip. Do you have any recommendations for a lightweight and waterproof tent that can fit one person?
